content.rendered:
Dr. Walker Shaffer is an optometrist focused on proactive, preventative eye care and long-term visual health. He is the co-owner of a growing multi-location practice where he integrates advanced diagnostic technology, nutrition, and innovative therapies, including light therapy, to help patients preserve vision before disease progression occurs.

Dr. Shaffer has a special interest in retinal health, dry eye disease, and performance-based vision care. He is the creator of the “Retinal Reserve” concept, a framework designed to shift eye care from reactive treatment to early intervention and functional preservation.

Outside of clinical practice, he is actively involved in educating both patients and fellow providers on modern, medicine-forward approaches to eye care.

Zip Code: 84043

Email: drshaffer@eyecareoflehi.com

Website: www.eyecareoflehi.com

content.rendered:
Chad J. Jackson is native of Michigan. He attended Michigan State University and earned his BS in Nutritional Science. He then went on to Illinois College of Optometry for his OD in 1998. After graduation he went back to his hometown of Clare, MI where he practiced for several years before moving to Rockford, MI and taking over Bella Vista Family Eye Care in 2005. Dr. Jackson has always enjoyed the role nutrition plays in our everyday health. He attended a Culinary Medicine class and became interested in food as medicine and the American College of Lifestyle Medicine (ACLM). He joined ACLM in 2021 and became board certified in Lifestyle Medicine in 2023. He incorporates the pillars of lifestyle medicine into his treatment plans for eyecare. He has put together a hands-on cooking class where professionals, patients, and the community may enroll to learn more about cooking for eye health.

Additionally, Dr. Jackson is also a Clinical Adjunct at Michigan College of Optometry (since spring 2023), a member of the Rockford Chamber of Commerce, Rockford Lions Club, Michigan Optometric Association, and American Optometric Association. He is currently co-pastor of his church and acting congregational CFO.

content.rendered:
Dr. J. Matt Gentry is a primary eye care specialist and the owner of The Eye Gallery. Dr. Gentry performs comprehensive eye examinations, which includes eyeglass and contact lens prescriptions, as well as evaluating the eye for the presence of dry eye disease, cataracts, glaucoma, age-related macular degeneration, diabetic retinopathy, hypertensive retinopathy, amblyopia and many other types of ocular pathology. Dr. Gentry earned his O.D. degree in 2004 from Northeastern State University Oklahoma College of Optometry graduating Summa Cum Laude. He then completed a residency in Primary Eye Care through the University of Houston College of Optometry. Dr. Gentry is a member of the Oklahoma Association of Optometric Physicians (OAOP) and the American Optometric Association (AOA). Dr. Gentry loves spending time with his wife Vickie and his four children. In his spare time, he enjoys audiobooks, exploring hiking trails, gardening, physical fitness, and discovering new ways to provide his patients with clear vision and healthy eyes.

title.rendered: Kaleb Abbott, OD, MS, FAAO, FOWNS

content.rendered:
Kaleb Abbott is an optometrist and assistant professor of ophthalmology at the University of Colorado School of Medicine. He is affiliated with both the Dry Eye Clinic and the Center for Ocular Inflammation, where he specializes in complex ocular surface diseases and participates in clinical trials and research related to these conditions.

In addition to his clinical and research roles, he serves as vice-president for the Ocular Wellness and Nutrition Society, is Chair of the Nutrition, Disease Prevention, and Wellness Special Interest Group (SIG) for the American Academy of Optometry (AAO), and is a member of the advisory council for the Academic Medical Center Optometry AAO SIG. He also holds a position on the editorial advisory board for Modern Optometry and Optometry360 and is a graduate of the AAO Flom Leadership Academy. Furthermore, he hosts the Dry Eye and Ocular Surface Disease section of the Clinical Podcast Series through the American Academy of Optometry Foundation. In 2024, he was recognized as a “One-to-Watch” by Modern Optometry.

In 2019, Kaleb co-founded SunSnap Kids, a start-up that won first place in the inaugural Bright Ideas Pitch Competition in 2022 and third place in the Optometry Innovation Awards in 2023. He recently sold the majority of the company to focus more on his clinical and research responsibilities at the University of Colorado.

When he’s not seeing patients, conducting research, or working on SunSnap Kids, Dr. Abbott lectures on ocular surface diseases, writes articles, and serves as a medical reviewer for multiple journals, including The Ocular Surface and Optometry and Vision Science. He resides in Denver, CO, with his wife, daughter, and newborn twins.

Clinic Name: University of Colorado School of Medicine

Address: 1675 Aurora Court
City: Aurora
State: Colorado
Zip Code:

Phone: (720) 848-2020
Email: kalebabbottOD@gmail.com

Website: som.cuanschutz.edu/Profiles/Faculty/Profile/36594

content.rendered:
We know that proper nutrition is vital for function of all bodily functions, especially vision. Nutrition plays a role in all parts of the eye from the lids and lashes, to the tear film, cornea, vitreous, retina, optic nerve and occipital lobes of the brain. Proper nutrition can slow the impact of some progressive eye diseases such as glaucoma, cataracts and macular degeneration. Using nutrition to support your vision system will keep you seeing far into the future!

Trained in England at the University of Manchester and Manchester Royal Eye Hospital, she has been providing modern, patient-centered care since 2011. Licensed in both the UK and Ontario, her clinical interests include glaucoma, myopia control, and contact lenses—with a strong emphasis on the role of nutrition in preventing and managing ocular disease. Dr. Kokoshko believes that supporting visual health starts from the inside out.

David is the clinical lead at the multi award winning independent eye care practice, David Burghardt Vision Care in the village of Nettleham, near the historic cathedral city of England. For almost 40 years, David and his expert team have been dedicated to providing excellent patient care to the people of Lincolnshire and beyond.

David was in the first cohort of UK optometrist to earn independent prescribing rights and is a fellow of the British Contact Lens Association. As well as being a skilled contact lens practitioner, he has an interest in the cornea and anterior eye, dry eye, glaucoma and nutrition.

content.rendered:
Dr. Kavitha Jayachandran (Dr. Jaya to her patients) was born and raised in Edmonton, AB. She received her Bachelors of Science with distinction in 2007 from the University of Alberta. She then attended Pennsylvania College of Optometry at Salus University with a Presidential Scholarship. She completed her Doctorate in Optometry with academic honors in 2011. During her time in Optometry school, Dr. Jaya became a member of the prestigious Beta Sigma Kappa Honors Society and the Golden Key International Honors Society. She also completed training as an intern with various specialties including contact lens, refractive surgery consultations and post operative management, ocular disease and low vision, at well renowned facilities such as the Newington VA hospital, South Carolina VA hospital, Eye Doctors of Washington and the Eye Institute in Philadelphia, PA. She completed an advanced certification in pediatrics as well, which helped her gain valuable experience in vision therapy, binocular vision, infant vision and special populations.

During her schooling she was honored to take part in a mission trip to provide eye care to the underprivileged villages in Belize. More recently, she had the pleasure of participating in volunteer trips to Malawi, Africa and Cuenca, Ecuador to provide eye care to underserved schools. Over the years, her work experience, personal experience and volunteer work sparked an interest in functional medicine and preventative care. She brought this passion to her practice, Glow Optometry and Wellness along with specialties such as myopia management, dry eye and specialty contact lenses. She is devoted to creating a healthier, happier future for her patients.

content.rendered:
Dr Cheryl Letheren graduated from Western University in London ON, Canada in 1987 with a degree in physiology and then graduated from the University of Waterloo, Optometry program in 1991. Part of her clinical education was with Salus Univeristy (then PCO) in 1990. Cheryl is currently in private practice in London Ontario for the last 33 years and provides full-scope Optometry including specialty contact lenses, myopia control, visual processing rehabilitation, brain injury rehabilitation and dry eye. She is currently a Fellow with the American Academy of Optometry for 27 years and is in the process of earning her fellowships with OVDRA, OEP and NORA. Cheryl also works in brain injury rehabilitation and helped to found the first and only Multi-disciplinary Vision Rehab program in Canada with her colleagues in PT, OT and SLP more than 17 years ago. She was an early adaptor for laser vision correction, as an Optometric Director at TLC laser centres from 1995-2000. Cheryl has participated on many boards and volunteered for many Optometric Committees in the past and is currently on the Board of Directors for COETF, the Canadian Optometric Education Trust Fund, the BIALR, the Brain Injury Association of London and Region, and NORA, the Neuro-Optometric Rehabilitation Association. Cheryl has spoken internationally on a variety of Optometric topics and teaching is a passion of hers. Continued learning is also a passion, as Cheryl is currently enrolled in an undergraduate Psychology Degree program at Western and plans to complete her Master’s by 2027. As she ages, Cheryl has become more and more interested in holistic health for herself and her patients and plans on taking more courses in nutrition and wellness.

Dr. Walker graduated from the University of Waterloo School of Optometry in 2012. After practicing in a variety of clinical settings shortly after graduation, he developed a passion for preventative and rehabilitative eye care. He pursued additional training in the areas of vision therapy, neuro-visual rehabilitation and ocular nutrition. He completed his fellowship with the Ocular Wellness & Nutrition Society (OWNS) in 2021 and the College of Optometrists in Vision Development (COVD) in 2023. These two areas of focus are complementary to his passion for protecting and maintaining optimal vision throughout life.

Dr. Walker works as a solo practitioner at Inspired Eyes Optometry, providing comprehensive primary eye care with a focus on preventing eye disease through nutrition and lifestyle recommendations. In addition, he practices with the team at Okanagan Vision Therapy, focusing on visual rehabilitation and binocular vision.

content.rendered:
Dr. Bruce Lidkea was born and raised in Fort Frances. He received his Doctor of Optometry degree from Indiana University in 1994.⁣ Dr. Lidkea’s training included a 4 month Ocular Disease rotation at the F.D.R. Veterans Administration Hospital in Montrose, New York and a 4 month Ocular Disease/Pediatric rotation at the White River Apache Reservation Public Health Service Hospital in White River, Arizona. Upon graduation He joined the practice of his father, Dr. R Lidkea as an associate. He became a partner in 1997.⁣

During the Summer of 2004, He was made Adjunct Faculty of the University of Waterloo School of Optometry by supervising 4th year externs during their Primary Care/Ocular Disease clinical rotations. Lidkea Optometry has since become a sought after rotation for students due to the quality of education provided. We are very proud of having over 30 ‘Lidkea Optometry Extern Alumni’ scattered across Canada and the US. ⁣

He has been President of the Fort Frances Chamber of Commerce (1998).⁣ He is a former assistant coach for the FFHS Muskie Boys soccer program. He is also very involved with Bagpiping. Dr. Lidkea has been a member of the Fort Frances Highlanders Pipes and Drums since 1996 and has been Pipe Major (musical director) from 2001 to present.
Dr. Bruce Lidkea held a seat on the boards of directors of The Ontario Association of Optometrists for 4 years. During this time he served as both the Education and the Advocacy Board Lead and was chairperson for the practice management initiative to develop a Staffing Manual template.⁣

Dr. Bruce Lidkea has had the privilege to lecture regionally, provincially and national on a variety of topics including practice management and the treatment and management of ocular disease.⁣

On July 8th, 2023 He was awarded his Fellowship with the Canadian College of Specialties in Optometry in the area of Neuro-rehabilitation.

content.rendered:
Dr. Ann has been working in eyecare for decades. Each of her 4 children have found their niche in eyecare too. One is an optician; one is a corneal surgeon and two are optometrists. The two O.D.’s now practice with her husband and herself, allowing her to expand her work in dry eye. In her “retirement” she is looking forward to expanding her Dry Eye Institute of Wisconsin delving more into nutrition and dry eye and expanding her use of IPL and LLLT in her clinic. She has always taken an interest in nutrition as a diagnosed celiac for the last 28 years, marveling at how her deep dive into nutrition decades ago has served her and her patients well, especially in her work with ocular surface disease. She looks forward to continuing to be a life-long learner!

Dr. Schuster grew up in the Twin Cities and received his Bachelor’s degree in Biology with honors from Bethel University in St. Paul, Minnesota. He went on to graduate magna cum laude with his Doctorate in Optometry from Southern College of Optometry in Memphis, Tennessee. While in Optometry school, Dr. Schuster received both educational and clinical awards and was also a member of Beta Sigma Kappa International Optometric Honor Society, the College of Optometrists in Vision Development, and the Optometric Private Practice Club. His clinical experience includes externship rotations at Memphis VA Medical Center in Memphis, Tennessee and Coldwater Vision Center in Coldwater, Mississippi, where he focused on primary eye care and ocular disease. Dr. Schuster is a member of the Wisconsin and American Optometric Associations and participates in the InfantSee program. Dr. Schuster provides comprehensive eye exams for glasses and contact lenses to patients of all ages. His clinical interests include the treatment, management, and prevention of ocular diseases, specialty dry eye care, co-management of cataract and LASIK surgeries, and the fitting of specialty contact lenses, especially to reduce myopia progression. Aside from Optometry, Dr. Schuster enjoys spending time with his wife and four children, singing and playing music, participating in a variety of sports, and relaxing in the great outdoors.

content.rendered:
Dr. Sharon Williams is a board certified optometrist with over three decades of experience in diagnosing and managing complex ocular conditions. She spent the majority of her career at Virginia Mason Franciscan Eye Care Associates and has recently transitioned to a small private eye care practice.

After graduating Magna Cum Lauda with a Bachelor of Science in Biology from St John Fisher College in Rochester, NY she moved to Columbus, Ohio to attend the Ohio State University College of Optometry. Her quest to further her training and work in a multidisciplinary setting compelled her to complete an ocular disease residency at Veterans Administration Medical Center in Cleveland, OH. During her residency, she did rotations at the Cleveland Sight Center in the Low Vision Clinic, at Case Western Reserve College of Medicine Specialty Contact Lens Clinic and at Ocular Prosthetics Clinic. Curiosity and a lifelong passion for learning continues to push her to stay up to date on the latest advancements in traditional Western medicine and also to explore integrative wellness practices. In 2017, she completed a year-long program at the Functional Medicine Coaching Academy to become a certified Functional Medicine Health Coach (FM-CHC).

Dr Williams has annually received an award for “Patient Experience” from her previous health care organization, consistently ranking above the 96th percentile nationally. She has also been a past president of the Pierce County Optometry Society. Advocating for accessible healthcare, Dr Williams has shared her expertise both locally, and internationally. Through Volunteer Optometric Services to Humanity (VOSH), she’s provided medical care, training, and optical product donations to underserved communities in Africa, Mexico, and Peru. She has also volunteered locally at two charity health clinics.

She is a proud mom of three young adults and you can often find her out in nature enjoying outdoor activities, gardening and traveling.

content.rendered:
Dr. Britt is the founder and owner of Eyecare Rockwall. She is a Diplomate with the American Board of Optometry. After receiving a Bachelor of Science Degree in Vision Sciences, with a minor in Math and Chemistry, Dr. Britt graduated from Northeastern State University College of Optometry in Tahlequah, Oklahoma, where she had extensive clinical experience working with the Indian Health Services. She has been in private practice Optometry in the Lake Ray Hubbard area for 33 years.

Dr. Britt is a member of the American Optometric Association, the Texas Optometric Association, and Beta Sigma Kappa Optometric Honors Fraternity. She holds licenses in Texas and Oklahoma and holds current Laser Certification in Oklahoma. Dr. Britt has been presented with the prestigious Helen Keller Award for Sight Preservation by The Lions Club for her work with the Rowlett Chapter, volunteering for over 17 years. She was also been awarded The Irvin M. Borish Award for Clinical Excellence during her time in Tahlequah.

Dr. Britt loves working with adolescents and geriatrics, and she also loves the everyday challenges of emergency eye care and hard-to-fit, specialty & medically necessary contact lens services. Dr. Britt has long witnessed in her patients the implications of lifestyle mismanagement with corresponding eye disease. She has recently added her passion for “proactive prevention and wellness” to her practice with the overwhelmingly positive response from her patients who are tired of what they perceive “healthcare” has become – “sick care” and “degeneration management”.

Born and raised in North Carolina, Dr. Meagan Embrey earned her Doctorate of Optometry from the University of Alabama at Birmingham before completing a residency at the W.G. Hefner VA Hospital in Salisbury, NC. With over a decade of experience practicing full-scope optometry, she is dedicated to providing comprehensive eye care while emphasizing the critical connection between vision and whole-body health.

Dr. Embrey’s passion lies in helping patients understand how their overall health impacts their eyes and well-being. As a Certified Integrative Health Coach and Certified Personalized Nutrition Practitioner, she goes beyond traditional eye care, guiding patients through individualized health coaching programs that empower them to make sustainable nutrition and lifestyle choices. She believes in treating each patient like family, fostering strong relationships, and educating them on the steps to achieve optimal health.

content.rendered:
Dr. Kara Foster is a passionate and forward-thinking optometrist dedicated to comprehensive patient care that goes beyond vision correction. With years of experience in primary eye care and ocular disease management, Dr. Kara believes in an integrative approach to healthcare—helping patients understand the crucial connection between eye health, nutrition, and overall well-being.

Recognizing that optometrists often see patients more regularly than their primary care doctors, Dr. Kara has made it a mission to educate and empower patients about lifestyle-based prevention for conditions like age-related macular degeneration, glaucoma, and diabetic eye disease. Inspired by the works of Dr. Michael Greger, Dr. Joel Fuhrman, and the Blue Zones research, Dr. Kara incorporates evidence-based nutrition and lifestyle recommendations into patient care, ensuring long-term vision health and improved quality of life.

Beyond the clinic, Dr. Kara is passionate about volunteering, having provided eye care to underserved communities both locally and internationally. A firm believer in prevention over intervention, she strives to make every patient interaction an opportunity to inspire healthier choices that preserve both eyesight and longevity.

title.rendered: Kenneth Daniels, OD FAAO Diplomate American Board of Optometry​

content.rendered:
Dr. Daniels and his associates believe in a holistic approach to eye and systemic healthcare merge natural medicine concepts with nutrition and medical guidelines.

Dr. Daniels is an Adjunct Assistant Clinical Professor in the Cornea and Specialty Contact lens Service and National Eye Institute Clinical Investigator at the Pennsylvania College of Optometry (PCO) where he was also the Director of Contact Lens Research and in PCO’s Centers for International Studies. He is a Fellow in the American Academy of Optometry (FAAO) Diplomate of the American Board of Optometry. He is a graduate of Temple University and the New England College of Optometry.

He has graduate training from an ophthalmology department in a Harvard affiliated health plan and the Yale – Gesell Institute of Child Development. He was the Director of Clinical Research for Allergan Pharmaceuticals and has collaborated in several research projects in Sydney, Australia at the University of New South Wales. He is a series editor for an ophthalmic text and contributing editor professional journals and has a published textbook pertaining to Contact Lenses. Dr. Daniels has written many professional articles and several book chapters in the topics of Cataract, Ocular Disease , Refractive Surgery, Contact Lens Lenses, and Dry Eye.

Dr. Daniels is the President / CEO of Hopewell – Lambertville Eye Associates a private group practice with a high volume of specialty contact lens, refractive surgical, medical-therapeutic eyecare, pathology care and surgical co – management with a Wills Eye affiliated practice. The office is a certified preceptor site for 4th year optometric students from the Pennsylvania College of Optometry, New England College of Optometry, and SCCO Ketchum College of Optometry. Our offices are actively involved in clinical research projects and pre – market launches of new contact lens and medical device products.

content.rendered:
Neda Gioia, OD, CNS®, FOWNS®, is the founder of Integrative Vision and the SEEHealth™ program. As a seasoned optometrist, Dr. Gioia has always been passionate about delivering exceptional eye and vision care. Upon learning more about the importance of whole-body health and the power of the body to heal itself through proper nutrition and lifestyle, she pursued further education and training in functional medicine and nutrition. Her goal is to enhance and transform the standard of care for eye and vision health within her practice and beyond.

Dr. Gioia earned an undergraduate degree from Rutgers University and a doctorate in optometry at SUNY College of Optometry. She completed a certified nutrition specialist program (CNS®) through the University of Western States, a fellowship in the Ocular Wellness and Nutrition Society, and the Functional Medicine Certification Program (IFMCP) through the Institute for Functional Medicine, which is the gold standard in functional medicine training.

In addition to her clinical work, Dr. Gioia is currently the President of the Ocular Wellness and Nutrition Society (OWNS) with a global mission of creating more awareness of the role of functional medicine and nutrition in eye health.

title.rendered: Mila Ioussifova, OD, CNS, FAAO, FOWNS​

content.rendered:
Dr. Mila Ioussifova is the proud owner of South Waterfront Eye Care, a boutique-style private practice she founded in 2013 in Portland, OR. She is recognized for her expertise in advanced dry eye treatments, nutrition and wellness, and aesthetic services.

Dr. Ioussifova graduated with honors from the New England College of Optometry and completed a residency program in community health and ocular disease in Boston, Massachusetts. She is a fellow of the American Academy of Optometry (AAO) and a lecturer at the academy and other national meetings. Dr. Ioussifova is on the board of directors as the education chair for the Oregon Optometric Physicians Association. She is also involved in state advocacy and leadership in the AAO Anterior Segment Section. She served as an adjunct clinical faculty member at the Pacific University College of Optometry and an examiner for the National Board of Examiners in Optometry. She is also a Certified Nutrition Specialist (CNS) accredited by the American Nutrition Association (ANA) and a fellow of the Ocular Nutrition and Wellness Society (OWNS).

Clinic Name: South Waterfront Eye Care, INC

Address: 3615 SW RIVER PKWY
City: Portland
State: Oregon
Zip Code: 97239

Phone: (971) 229-08207
Email: Dr.Mila@swec.us

Website: www.Southwaterfronteyecare.com

content.rendered:
Dr. Dunn graduated from Pacific University College of Optometry in 2013 and then completed a residency at the Roseburg VA in Roseburg, Oregon specializing in geriatric care and ocular disease. After completing her residency, she opened Gatti Vision in Portland, Oregon. In addition, she became an assistant professor at Pacific University College of Optometry.

Dr. Dunn is passionate about preventative eye care as well as dry eye. She works hard to ensure she is staying up to date on her education to ensure patients get the best care possible.

Since the start of optometry school, Dr. Dunn has been highly active in organized optometry. As a student she represented her school on the AOSA Board of Trustees, and in her fourth year of school was elected the Vice President of all the optometry schools in North America. Since graduation she has served on several committees within the American Optometric Association and served five years on the board for the Oregon Optometric Physicians Association.

Dr. Dunn was awarded Young OD of the year for Oregon in 2018, she received the National American Optometric Association Media Advocacy Award in 2022 and the Social Media Award for Oregon in 2023.

Clinic Name: Gatti Vision

Address: 15405 SW 116th ave Unit 204
City: King City
State: Oregon
Zip Code: 97224

Phone: (971) 371-3927
Email: amber.gatti@gmail.com

Website: www.gattivision.com

content.rendered:
Dr. Jahnke is a dedicated optometrist committed to delivering comprehensive, patient-focused eye care. Practicing full-scope optometry, Dr. Jahnke provides thorough, personalized eye exams with a focus on diagnosing and managing a wide range of ocular conditions. With a special interest in dry eye disease and specialty contact lens fittings, she tailors treatments to fit each patient’s unique visual and lifestyle needs.

Believing that true eye health extends beyond the exam room, Dr. Jahnke integrates nutrition and systemic health education into patient care, offering holistic recommendations to support long-term ocular wellness. From managing complex contact lens cases to creating customized dry eye treatment plans, Dr. Jahnke is passionate about helping patients achieve optimal vision and overall health.

content.rendered:
Ansel Johnson, OD is founder and clinical director of Vision Salon Eye Care Associates in Blue Island, IL. A graduate of the New England College of Optometry, he serves as adjunct professor and externship faculty at Chicago College of Optometry, NECO, Indiana University and Kentucky College of Optometry. He has staff privileges at Advocate Trinity Hospital in Chicago, Illinois. His office focuses on connecting patients eye care and eye health with their overall health and lifestyle. As a CDC Diabetes Prevention Program Certified Lifestyle Coach, he helps patients reduce their risks of vision impairment with lifestyle coaching and cutting-edge eye care. His office was the first to incorporate a nationally certified diabetes education program in an eye care office.

He is the founder and CEO of the 501c3 Healthy Living With A Vision Foundation dedicated to eliminating health care disparities in historically underserved communities. As a national speaker and key opinion leader, Dr Johnson is dedicated to educating the eye care community and the world community to empowering patients to live their healthiest life with good vision.

Clinic Name: Vision Salon Eye Care Associates

Address: 12812 Western Ave.
City: Blue Island
State: Illinois
Zip Code: 60406

Phone: (708) 385-0013
Email: info@visionsalon.com

Website: www.visionsalon.com

title.rendered: Pamela Lowe, OD, FAAO, Dipl ABO​

content.rendered:
Dr. Lowe is currently Director/President of Professional Eye Care Center, Incorporated, a private practice she founded in 1992 on Chicago’s Northwest Side. She received her Bachelor of Science degree from Loyola University of Chicago in 1984 and graduated with honors from the Illinois College of Optometry in 1988. The college named her the Alumnus of the Year in 2002.

Dr. Lowe is active in organized optometry and is a Past President of the Illinois Optometric Association. Dr. Lowe was awarded Illinois’s Young Optometrist of the Year in 1995 and Optometrist of the Year in 2006. She has been a career long member of the American Optometric Association and currently serves Chair of the AOA Strategic Communications Committee and Keyperson for AOA Advocacy; she is a Fellow with the American Academy of Optometry and Diplomate of the American Board of Optometry.

title.rendered: Dr. Ingryd Lorenzana, FAAO, FOVDRA, CBHP – Neuro-Optometrist​

content.rendered:
Dr. Ingryd Lorenzana is an international award-winning optometrist and vision specialist, the innovator of the groundbreaking patent-pending CogniViiSION® “TRAIN the BRAIN not JUST the EYES “Method to provide INDIVIDUALIZED VISION OPTIMIZATION to people of ALL ages using an integrative whole BODY – BRAIN and HOLISTIC approach with a foundation pillar of nutrition.

Dr. Ingryd, as her patient’s call her, has a great passion for her work, and with good reason. She truly is a testament of when she, the “patient” had to become the “doctor” to find solutions for her own ailments. Most of her life, she struggled with reading and attention issues. She loved to learn, but had great difficulty with being a slow reader and didn’t know why. She always had to work harder and study longer than any of her friends just to keep up if she was to succeed. Most people are unaware that 90% of the Brain is VISUAL, for this reason VISION impacts every aspect of our life.

In kids, VISION, impacts their development, ability to read, learn and pay attention. In brain injury or concussion patient, VISION is the most accelerated path to recovery. In senior and elderly, VISION is the most effective manner to maintain vital brain function and health optimization. A healthy and optimized vision system leads to greater quality of life that propels individual success to people of all ages in everything they do. Call us to get your VISUAL PATHWAY to SUCCESS ACTIVATED TODAY!”

Clinic Name: Brain Vision Institute

Address: 2 Executive Ct, Suite 3
City: South Barrington
State: Illinois
Zip Code: 60010

Phone: (847) 891-8003

Email: dringryd@gmail.com

Website: www.Brain-Vision.org

content.rendered:
Dr. Carrie Roitstein has 30 years of experience in the Optometry industry. Previously the Managing Doctor for Spex, she is now a proud partner of Glimpse Vision as an independent Optometrist – working with the most talented and knowledgeable Opticians in both Hinsdale, IL and Chicago’s Gold Coast. As a board certified Personalized Nutrition Practitioner and Inegrative Health Coach, Dr. Carrie combines her expertise of medical wellness with the latest technology diagnosing and treating eye conditions. Additionally, she works with the newest options in specialty contact lenses, exclusive independent eyewear designers and prescription lenses in the industry. She is a graduate of the Illinois College of Optometry and the Institute for Integrative Nutrition and member of the American Nutrition Association.

Dr. Carrie strives to introduce her patients to safer and healthier eyecare products for the treatment and prevention of eye disease. She offers co-management services for LASIK, cataract surgery and other necessary and elective surgical intervention. She also specializes in treating Dry Eye Disease offering all natural and non-toxic products including supplements, lid treatments, lubricating drops and more. She also offers expertise in specialty contact lenses including custom multifocals, hybrid and scleral lenses for the treatment of corneal disease. She welcomes new patients of all ages.

In addition to providing her patients with premium eyecare, she loves to give back with eyecare mission trips to Mexico. She also bike rides 111 miles annually on the Owl Ride – supporting the local Jackson Chance Foundation.

Clinic Name: Glimpse Vision

Address: 12 E First Street
City: Hinsdale
State: Illinois
Zip Code: 60521

Phone: (630) 325-2020
Email: hinsdale@glimpsevision.com

Website: www.glimpsevision.com

content.rendered:
Dr. Thieme graduated from Brigham Young University in 1991 and completed optometry school at the University of California, Berkeley in 1994. He opened Meridian Family Eyecare in 1995, and moved the practice to the present location in 2009.

His whole-body approach to eye disease and systemic condions (diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, high cholesterol, and auto-immune diseases) is a focus in his work.

Dr. Thieme enjoys all aspects of eye care including eye exams for glasses and contact lenses, along with eye health care for glaucoma, diabetic eye care, macular degeneration, and surgical care of cataract and LASIK patients.

Dr. Thieme lives in Meridian with his wife, Iris and their children. He loves boating, fishing, hiking and all outdoor activities.

content.rendered:
Dr. Jeffrey Anshel is a 1975 graduate from the Illinois College of Optometry. He has written numerous articles and nine books regarding nutritional influences on vision and computer vision concerns. Dr. Anshel is the principal of Corporate Vision Consulting, where he addresses nutrition and eye health, as well as digital eyestrain. He lectures internationally to ergonomic professionals and eyecare providers on vision health topics. Dr. Anshel is a Fellow of the American Academy of Optometry and Founder and Past President of the Ocular Wellness and Nutrition Society. He maintains a consulting business in Kapaa, Hawaii.

Prevention is at the heart of everything Dr. Munson does, and she firmly believes in empowering her patients with knowledge, natural remedies and procedures, and measurable outcomes to ensure their potential is always ahead of the game. “You should never be held captive by your vision,” she says. At Fort Collins Family Eye Care, Dr. Munson has built a network of providers inside and outside of her own industry who cultivate an attitude and culture of prevention. This network of like-minded providers creates a circle of safety around patient care and best outcomes.

With a Master’s degree in Medical Nutrition and an M.B.A. in Healthcare Management, Dr. Smith believes that the integrated approach to healthcare will best help her patients to better maintain both their eye and general health status and improve their quality of life.
In addition to being in private practice for over 30 years, Dr. Smith has contributed to professional journals and developed optical assistant and ABO training courses online. She has also travelled to Kenya and Ghana on eye care mission trips and has served as an Expert Examiner for the California State Board of Optometry and as a board member of the ACCC Optometric Society.

content.rendered:
Kathryn Waters is a native of northern Wisconsin and graduate of the Illinois College of Optometry. She moved to California for extensive training at the Palo Alto VA Medical Center and after fifteen years in the Bay Area, she has followed her heart to beautiful Montecito in Santa Barbara County. Dr. Waters offers a vast array of knowledge and proficiency in vision care for our community including ocular nutrition and contact lenses. Non-profit work has been a key component of her career, including two years of service as the first optometrist for the “See Well to Learn” program with Prevent Blindness Northern California. You will find “Kate” enjoying her free time cooking for her family, friends, and two German Shepherds. She is an outdoor enthusiast, avid reader, and is eager to share stories about these adventures with you.
